name: Jotai MCP Server
what_it_does: Official MCP server from the Jotai team (jotaijs org) for Jotai — the primitive and flexible state management library for React. Enables AI agents to access Jotai documentation, API references, and usage patterns for building React applications with Jotai's atomic state model.
use_cases: Accessing Jotai documentation and API references from coding agents
Getting accurate Jotai usage patterns for React development agents
Querying Jotai atom patterns, derived atoms, and async state management
Integrating Jotai knowledge into AI-assisted React development workflows
not_for: Teams using Redux, Zustand, Recoil, or other state management libraries
Applications needing centralized state mutation patterns (Jotai is decentralized/atomic)
best_when: A React developer using Jotai wants AI coding assistants to have authoritative, up-to-date knowledge about Jotai APIs, patterns, and best practices — directly from the Jotai team.
avoid_when: You don't use Jotai. This is a documentation/knowledge MCP for a specific library — not a general-purpose development tool.

auth.notes: No authentication required — documentation server with no external API calls.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Completely free — open source documentation server
pricing.notes: Free open source from jotaijs org. Documentation is served locally or from the Jotai docs site.

known_agent_gotchas: Documentation MCP — value is high-quality official Jotai docs, not runtime operations
Jotai v2 API differs significantly from v1 — ensure docs match your installed version
Jotai ecosystem includes many extensions (jotai/utils, jotai-tanstack-query, etc.) — coverage varies
Official from jotaijs but relatively niche — verify docs freshness against latest Jotai releases
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local documentation server. No credentials. No external data transmission. Safe to use in all environments.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local documentation server. No credentials. No external access. Zero security risk.
